verses.text: Este salmo se enseñaba a los jóvenes como parte de su educación. • Con respecto al tiempo: ver **2 Sam 8.13-14** . • Aram-naharaim y Aram-Zobá se refieren a los arameos del noroeste de Mesopotamia y Siria (véase nota de estudio sobre Gn 24.10 ). • La melodía “Lirio del Testimonio” es desconocida hoy en día.
Cuando el pueblo de Dios experimenta su disciplina, reafirma su lealtad y espera la respuesta de Dios.

vino (hebreo yayin) que nos hizo tambalear: yayin, el término hebreo genérico que significa "vino", a menudo se asocia con la embriaguez ( **2 Sam 13.28;** **Is 5.11;** **28.7-8;** cp. **Salm 104.15** ; **Dt 14.26** ; **Isa 55.1** ), simbolizaba el juicio de Dios sobre los malvados ( **Salm 75.8** ; **Je 51.7** ).

Dios levantó una insignia sobre su pueblo para servir como un punto de reunión y como una fuente de orgullo y confianza. • El temor de Dios que conduce a la sabiduría ( **111.10** ) no es debilidad; proporciona la perspectiva y la fuerza necesarias para actuar correctamente.
El salmista ora por el rescate, basándose en la relación especial entre Dios y su pueblo.
Sichêm y Succoth representan el corazón de Israel.
El Señor responde con un mensaje de promesa. Comparte su tierra con su pueblo como recompensa de guerra.
El Señor responde con un mensaje de promesa. Comparte su tierra con su pueblo como recompensa de guerra.

Destruir la capital de un invasor derrotado (la ciudad fortificada) ayudaría a asegurar que no atacara de nuevo. La capital de Edom era Bosra, ubicada al sureste de Israel. Solo Dios podía conceder la victoria.

60:10 Wilt not thou, O God?.... This is an answer to the question, and is made by putting another, which tacitly contains in it an affirmation that God would do it. He has foretold the destruction of the Romish antichrist; he has said it shall be: he is faithful to his purposes, predictions, and promises; he is able to effect it; strong is the Lord that judgeth Babylon, Rev 18:10; He will put it into the hearts of the kings of the earth to hate her;

60:11 Give us help from trouble,.... To have trouble is the common lot of all men, but especially of the people of God. They have some troubles which others have not, arising from indwelling sin, Satan's temptations, and the hidings of God's face; and as for outward troubles, they have generally the greatest share of them, which are certain to them by the appointment of God, and the legacy of Christ; though they are needful and for their good, and lie in their way to heaven.

60:12 Through God we shall do valiantly,.... Or, "through the Word of the Lord", as the Targum; Christ, whose name is the Word of God, appearing at the head of his armies, in a vesture dipped in blood, and with a sharp sword proceeding out of his mouth, will inspire his people to fight valiantly under him; and who, in his name and strength, will get the victory over all their enemies, the beast, false prophets, and kings of the earth, and all under them;
